mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS lowers the short-rate discount component applied to equities; when the 3-month change in FEDFUNDS is negative, S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days are positive.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS was 0.15 percent in the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if triggered easing observations produce non-positive average 20-day S&P 500 returns or if the FEDFUNDS 3-month change has Rank IC below 0.03 in absolute value over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE combined with falling PAYEMS identifies accelerating labor-income contraction, which reduces expected household spending and earnings; this produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months and PAYEMS falls over the same 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 7.6 percent and PAYEMS was 134580 thousand in the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of historical walk-forward cases or if PAYEMS declines add no incremental predictive power after conditioning on UNRATE.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous 3-month improvement in RSAFS and HOUST signals that consumer demand and residential investment are bottoming, raising expected sales and cyclicals' earnings; this produces positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month change > 0 and HOUST 3-month change > 0 after both series declined over the prior 6 months
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS was 344649 million dollars and HOUST was 550 thousand units in the latest available snapshots
failure_condition: Reject if joint RSAFS-HOUST stabilization does not produce higher 60-day returns than periods with only one series improving, or if the interaction coefficient is not positive in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp fall in INDPRO signals immediate contraction in goods output, capacity use, and operating leverage, forcing earnings revisions lower; this produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls by more than 1.0% month over month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO was 103.5966 in the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO drops greater than 1.0% month over month are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered cases or if mean triggered returns are not below baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL decelerates while FEDFUNDS is already near zero, real debt burdens rise and monetary policy has limited room to offset deflation pressure; this produces negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL was 211.49 and FEDFUNDS was 0.15 percent in the latest available snapshots
failure_condition: Reject if the FEDFUNDS-near-zero and CPI-deflation trigger is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if CPIAUCSL deceleration predicts positive rather than negative returns in the ZLB subsample.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in HOUST below a recession threshold signals persistent weakness in residential construction, durable-goods demand, and bank collateral quality; this produces negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST was 550 thousand units in the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST crossings below 600 thousand units are not followed by lower 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-triggered months, or if the estimated return spread is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE is high but PAYEMS losses become less severe over 3 months, equities price the labor-market trough before unemployment peaks; this produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 7.0% and PAYEMS 3-month change improves relative to the prior 3-month change
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 7.6 percent in the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if high-UNRATE payroll-improvement triggers do not outperform high-UNRATE payroll-deterioration triggers over 20 trading days, or if the conditional return difference is negative in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A large negative change in GDPC1 confirms broad demand contraction that lowers aggregate revenues and profit margins; when real GDP growth is deeply negative, S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days are negative.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 was 11599.4 billions of chained 2017 dollars in the latest available quarterly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if GDPC1 growth below -3.0% is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations or if negative GDP growth has no negative predictive coefficient after walk-forward estimation.
